title: linum alpinum var. ruscinonense
intro: Linum alpinum var. ruscinonense, also known as alpine flax, is a plant species that belongs to the genus Linum and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of alpine flax include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain diseases, such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Alpine flax can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or tincture, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
